description: This lecture discusses the online bipartite matching problem, where vertices on the right side appear one at a time, and a matching must be made immediately upon their arrival. The goal is to compute a maximum matching without edge weights, aiming for a competitive ratio as close to one as possible. The lecture also highlights the limitations of deterministic algorithms in achieving a competitive ratio better than 1/2.
topic_summary: Lecture on online bipartite matching problem, focusing on immediate matching upon vertex arrival and competitive ratios. Discusses limitations of deterministic algorithms in achieving optimal matching.
